summary: A decent era‑era audience recording captures a stellar Dead show highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, ripping Truckin' and classic R&B covers.
review: Reviewers rave about the performance – Dark Star is described as “out there,” Truckin’ and the R&B transformations (Dancing in the Streets, Not Fade Away, Good Lovin’) are called fabulous, and even the early‑set “Dew” gets holy praise. The recording is noted as a decent, slightly different‑sounding audience capture from this period, with a pleasant vibe but no major flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Three reviews (two 5‑star, one 3‑star) are overwhelmingly positive about the music; recording quality is described as decent for the era, yielding an overall high rating with moderate confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note a pleasant audience vibe and a richer, almost stereo‑like dynamic compared to other tape sources; no serious dropouts or distortion mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set leads into expansive jams; classic rock/R&B covers are transformed with psychedelic flair, making the show feel like a creative peak.

band_member_comments.Bob: One reviewer mentions Bob was “tripping balls” at this show, implying an intense vocal/performance presence.
